sense.definition: a method of restarting play in soccer, rugby, and other ball sports, where a player kicks the ball without opposition from the opposing team

examples.x: The referee awarded a free kick to our team after a foul near the penalty area.
The soccer player lined up for the free kick, aiming for the top corner of the goal.
He took a quick free kick to catch the defenders off guard.
In rugby, a free kick is awarded for minor infractions.

sense.definition: a method of kicking the ball where it is dropped from the hands and kicked before it hits the ground

examples.x: He launched a high punt that traveled over 50 yards downfield.
The rugby player opted for a tactical punt to relieve pressure on their defensive line.
The quarterback practiced his punt technique to improve field position for the team.
She kicked a booming punt to clear the ball from their defensive zone.
The punter's accurate punt allowed the coverage team to down the ball inside the 10-yard line.

sense.definition: a kick where the ball is placed on the ground before being kicked, commonly used in sports like American football and rugby

examples.x: He lined up for a place kick to score the extra point.
The team practiced their place kicks during training.
They set up the ball for a place kick after the touchdown.
His powerful place kick sent the ball through the uprights.
The coach emphasized the importance of accuracy in place kicks.

sense.definition: (in sports) to advance with the ball toward the goal, aiming to score points or create opportunities for the team

examples.x: In basketball, the point guard's ability to drive through the defense is crucial for setting up scoring plays.
In the final minutes of the game, he used his speed to drive past the defenders and score a crucial basket.
During the basketball game, the point guard led a fast break, skillfully dribbling to drive towards the basket for a layup.
As the rugby team gained possession, they quickly formed a coordinated attack to drive towards the try line.

sense.definition: a pass or action by a player that helps a teammate score a point or goal

examples.x: He provided an assist that led to the winning goal.
Her quick thinking and assist helped the team tie the game.
She recorded three assists in last night's basketball game.
He is known for his vision and ability to make assists.
The team celebrated his assist, which secured their victory.

sense.definition: the act of defending a team's goal in sports such as soccer or hockey by preventing the opposing team from scoring

examples.x: Good goalkeeping requires quick thinking and agility.
His goalkeeping kept the score tied until the last minute.
The team's success was largely due to his excellent goalkeeping.

sense.definition: a pass or play from the side to the middle of the field to set up a scoring opportunity in games like soccer or hockey

examples.x: He made a perfect center to his teammate in front of the goal.
She practiced her centers to improve her accuracy.
His quick center caught the defense off guard.
She aimed her center toward the striker in the box.
The game-winning goal came from a well-timed center.

sense.definition: to try to take the ball from the players of the other team, usually by forcing them down, in sports such as American football or rugby

examples.x: The linebacker tackled the quarterback before he could make the pass.
She tackled her opponent with a clean hit, stopping the play.
The player hesitated and was tackled just short of the goal line.
He lunged to tackle the player but missed by inches.
The defender tackled him aggressively, earning a penalty for rough play.

sense.definition: to use a defensive maneuver to take the ball or puck away from an opponent

examples.x: She tackles cleanly to avoid a foul.
They have tackled effectively all match.
They were tackling hard throughout the second half.
He was tackling when the referee blew the whistle.

sense.definition: the line that marks the boundary between the playing field and the goal area in sports like soccer, American football, and rugby

examples.x: The ball must cross the goal line for a goal to be scored in soccer.
The player sprinted toward the goal line, hoping to score.
He kicked the ball, and it barely went over the goal line for a point.
The referee checked the replay to see if the ball crossed the goal line.
She celebrated as her shot crossed the goal line and secured a win for her team.

sense.definition: the act of catching or stopping the ball and returning it to prevent runs or base advances after the batter hits it in cricket or baseball

examples.x: His excellent fielding saved several runs in the game.
She focused on improving her fielding during practice.
The team's strong fielding made it difficult for the opponents to score.
They won the match thanks to their sharp fielding.
Her fielding was crucial to the team's defense.

sense.definition: (in sports) to prevent an opponent from scoring a goal or point

examples.x: The goalkeeper defended the net, stopping several powerful shots.
The defense team succeeded in defending their goal from any further attacks.
She was able to defend her position, preventing the attacker from scoring a point.
In the last quarter, they defended aggressively to maintain their advantage.
The defender quickly moved to defend against the opponent's attempt at a goal.

sense.definition: the line on the side of a soccer or rugby field that marks the boundary

examples.x: The ball went out of play over the touchline.
The coach gave instructions from the touchline.
He sprinted down the touchline to keep the ball in play.
Players stood ready to substitute near the touchline.
The assistant referee ran up and down the touchline during the match.

sense.definition: a defensive play that drives the ball away from the goal area

examples.x: The defender made a crucial clearance to relieve pressure on the defense.
His clearance reached the halfway line, allowing the team to regroup.
The team relies on her accurate clearances to maintain control of the game.
A well-timed clearance prevented the opposing team from scoring.
With seconds left on the clock, the defender's clearance secured the victory.

sense.definition: a quick offensive move in basketball or other sports where the team rapidly advances the ball to score before the defense can set up

examples.x: The team scored easily on a fast break.
He led the fast break with a quick pass to his teammate.
They practiced fast break drills during training.
The fast break caught the defense off guard.
Coaches emphasize the importance of speed and precision in a fast break.

sense.definition: an act of moving the ball along the ground with repeated slight touches or bounces, especially in soccer, hockey, and basketball
(in sports) to block or prevent an opponent from scoring a goal or point in a game

examples.x: The defender's slide tackle saved a potential goal-scoring opportunity.
The wrestler executed a perfect countermove to save a point in the wrestling match.
The outfielder's catch at the wall saved a home run from being scored.
The agile goalkeeper saved two penalty shots during the shootout.
The defender's quick reflexes saved a certain goal during the soccer match.
The tennis player saved match point with a perfectly executed serve.

sense.definition: a deep, forward pass or hit in a ball game, typically covering a significant distance down the field

examples.x: The soccer player executed a perfect bomb to the striker up front.
His powerful bomb sailed over the outfielder's head for a double.
The rugby team's strategy relied on accurate bombs to gain ground.
She hit a bomb to center field for a home run.
With seconds left in the game, the Hail Mary bomb was their last chance to score.

sense.definition: a type of shot in golf and similar sports where the ball is hit with a controlled spin to change its direction

examples.x: He practiced his cut to improve his game strategy.
He used a cut to steer the ball around the obstacle.
The golfer's cut avoided the water hazard.
The pool player's cut banked the ball into the pocket perfectly.
The cricket batsman's cut raced towards the boundary.

sense.definition: (in sports) to start a game or match by kicking the ball or puck

examples.x: The team that wins the coin toss will choose whether to kick off or receive the ball.
The referee blew the whistle, and the game kicked off.
The referee blew the whistle, and the teams kicked off the soccer match.
The team captain will have the honor of kicking off the inaugural game of the new stadium.
In rugby, the team that scores first gets to kick off to the opposing team.

sense.definition: (sports) a kicking technique where the ball is dropped and kicked just after it touches the ground

examples.x: The rugby player scored a dropkick from midfield.
He attempted a dropkick from 40 meters out.
The football kicker practiced his dropkick technique before the game.
She executed a perfect dropkick to score the winning points.
In soccer, a dropkick is used to clear the ball from the defensive zone.

sense.definition: a method of restarting play in soccer and similar sports where a player throws the ball back into play from the sideline

examples.x: The referee awarded a throw-in to the opposing team.
He took a quick throw-in to catch the defense off guard.
She practiced her throw-in technique to improve distance and accuracy.
The team lost possession after an illegal throw-in.
The referee signaled for a throw-in after the ball went out of bounds.

sense.definition: (baseball, cricket) to catch, stop, or pick up the ball and often to throw it to a teammate or back to the pitcher or bowler

examples.x: He fields ground balls at shortstop during practice.
She fields the ground ball smoothly and fires it to first base for the out.
The outfielder fields the ball cleanly and throws it to the cutoff man.
She fields the bunt and throws to first base for the out.
The team's success relies on their ability to field cleanly and make accurate throws under pressure.

sense.definition: (in sports such as soccer) to score a goal by kicking the ball

examples.x: During the match, she kicked the winning goal in the final seconds.
She kicked the decisive goal in the final minute, clinching the championship.
He kicked the match-winning goal just before the final whistle blew.
With the match on the line, he kicked a brilliant goal in the last moments of the game.

sense.definition: (in sports) an act of hitting the ball using a bat or racket
to achieve a particular score in a round of golf

examples.x: He managed to shoot a 72, just under par for the course.
After weeks of practice, she finally shot her best score of the season.
The golfer shot a 68, setting a new record for the tournament.
She shot a 75, which was good enough to qualify for the finals.
He’s been working on his swing to shoot a lower score next time.

sense.definition: to give the ball to a teammate by kicking, throwing, etc.

examples.x: He passed the ball to Sterling.
His intent was to pass the ball forward rather than knock it back.

sense.definition: to hit a ball in sports before it touches the ground, typically with a quick and controlled strike

examples.x: He volleyed the soccer ball into the net for a spectacular goal.
The goalkeeper swiftly volleyed away the incoming shot.
From a distance, he volleyed the basketball into the hoop.
The volleyball player deftly volleyed the ball to score a point.
Out of the bunker, he volleyed the golf ball onto the green.

sense.definition: the skill of making accurate or successful shots in sports like golf, tennis, or basketball

examples.x: She impressed everyone with her precise shotmaking in the basketball game.
Her shotmaking at the net was unmatched by any opponent.
The golfer's shotmaking from the bunker was a thing of beauty.
He practiced his shotmaking tirelessly to perfect his technique.
The golfer's shotmaking from the bunker was a thing of beauty.

sense.definition: the average number of runs scored by a batter per 100 balls faced

examples.x: His strike rate of 120 indicates he scores, on average, 120 runs from every 100 balls faced.
He aims to improve his strike rate by practicing power hitting.
His strike rate dipped in the latter half of the innings.
The coach encourages aggressive batting to increase the team's overall strike rate.
The opening batsman has an impressive strike rate in T20 cricket.

sense.definition: an uncontrolled ball available for any player to pick it up

examples.x: He dove for the loose ball to gain possession for his team.
The game intensified as players scrambled for the loose ball.
She quickly grabbed the loose ball and passed it to her teammate.
The defender snatched the loose ball and started a fast break.
The loose ball bounced unpredictably, creating chaos among the players.

sense.definition: a shot where the ball is hit immediately after it bounces

examples.x: He scored with a powerful half-volley from the edge of the box.
His half-volley left the goalkeeper with no chance to save it.
The half-volley requires precise timing and control.
His half-volley flew past the defenders and into the net.
The player executed a perfect half-volley, sending the ball into the top corner.

sense.definition: a tactic in sports where a player passes the ball to a teammate and immediately moves into a new position to receive a return pass

examples.x: They scored a goal with a perfect give-and-go play.
The basketball team used a give-and-go to create an open shot.
He initiated a give-and-go in soccer to advance down the field.
The player used a give-and-go to set up a scoring opportunity in lacrosse.
She executed a give-and-go with her teammate to break through the defense.

sense.definition: a quick and close-range transfer of the ball between players on the same team

examples.x: The midfielder made a short pass to his teammate.
She executed a perfect short pass to set up the goal.
Quick short passes can break down the opposition's defense.
they practiced short passes during training to improve teamwork.
the striker received a short pass and took a shot at goal.

sense.definition: the continued motion after hitting the ball with a bat, racket, or club

examples.x: His powerful swing included a perfect follow-through.
She practiced her follow-through to improve her accuracy.
His follow-through on the tennis court impressed the spectators.
She worked on her follow-through to ensure better control over her hits.
She noticed her follow-through was lacking and adjusted her technique.

sense.definition: to strategically position oneself between an opponent and their intended target, obstructing their movement or view

examples.x: In basketball, the player screened the defender to create space for his teammate to take a shot.
The offensive lineman screened the linebacker to give the quarterback more time to throw the ball.
The point guard screened his defender to give his teammate an open lane to drive to the basket in basketball.
The winger screened the opposing goalie, obstructing his view of the puck, leading to a goal in ice hockey.
The offensive tackle screened the defensive lineman, giving the quarterback a clear passing lane in American football.

sense.definition: the act of gaining possession of the ball from an opponent through legal means

examples.x: She executed a perfect steal from her opponent.
The defender made a crucial steal to stop the attack.
He used his speed to pull off the steal.
They relied on their defense to generate steals.
His steal in the final seconds secured the win for his team.

sense.definition: a strategy where each player defends an assigned area rather than a specific opponent

examples.x: The team switched to zone defense in the second half.
She excelled at playing zone defense.
They practiced zone defense during training.
Zone defense frustrated the opposing team's offense.
Zone defense requires strong communication between players.

sense.definition: (of ball games) to lose control of the ball, resulting in a missed opportunity to score

examples.x: The player turned over the ball, allowing the opposing team to gain an advantage.
The soccer team struggled after turning over possession in a critical moment.
The coach emphasized the importance of not turning over the ball in crucial situations.
Turning over the ball frequently cost the team valuable points.

sense.definition: the act of hitting a ball or object incorrectly or inaccurately

examples.x: Her mishit was evident when the tennis ball flew out of bounds.
The pop-up catch was due to the cricketer's mishit.
The rough terrain was the result of his mishit in golf.

sense.definition: a deceptive move in basketball and Amerian football where a player pretends to shoot or pass the ball to mislead the defender

examples.x: The quarterback used a pump fake to trick the defender and then threw a deep pass.
The player performed a pump fake, causing the defender to jump, and then drove to the basket.
With a quick pump fake, the receiver created space and caught the ball easily.
The pump fake caused the cornerback to bite, resulting in a big gain for the offense.
Using a pump fake, the player fooled the defender and passed to a teammate for a dunk.

sense.definition: to obstruct or hinder the progress or action of an opponent, typically in sports or competitive activities

examples.x: In basketball, the defender blocked the opponent's shot with a well-timed jump.
The goalkeeper blocked the striker's attempt to score a goal with a diving save.
The tennis player strategically blocked her opponent's powerful serve with a quick return.
In volleyball, the middle blocker successfully blocked the opponent's spike, earning a point for her team.
The boxer effectively blocked his opponent's punches, minimizing the impact of the attacks.

sense.definition: to advance while carrying the ball, typically in sports such as football, soccer, or rugby

examples.x: The quarterback decided to run the ball instead of passing it.
He ran the kick return to the 50-yard line.
The player ran the pass down the court for a layup.

sense.definition: a sport that combines elements of soccer and volleyball, played with a ball similar to a football and originating from South Korea

examples.x: Playing jokgu improves coordination and teamwork skills.
Jokgu requires agility and precise ball control.
He practices jokgu every afternoon after school.
Jokgu players use both their feet and heads to pass the ball.

sense.definition: a traditional Indian sport involving two teams where players try to tag opponents while holding their breath

examples.x: My brother plays kabaddi every weekend with his friends at the park.
She watched a kabaddi match for the first time and found it incredibly fast-paced.
In rural India, kabaddi tournaments are a popular form of entertainment.
The kabaddi player demonstrated remarkable skill in evading the opposing team.
Playing kabaddi helps improve endurance and mental alertness.

sense.definition: a goal resulting from when a player unintentionally scores a goal for the opposing team by sending the ball into their own net

examples.x: They lost the match due to a series of defensive mistakes, including an own goal.
The opposing team capitalized on the own goal to secure their victory.
Own goals can happen unexpectedly and change the course of a game.
She tried to clear the ball but ended up with an unfortunate own goal.
The defender's clearance deflected off another player and resulted in an own goal.

sense.definition: a pass in sports, typically football or rugby, where the ball is passed sideways or backwards to a teammate

examples.x: He made a quick lateral pass to his teammate on the wing.
The rugby player's lateral pass set up a scoring opportunity.
They used lateral passes to maintain possession of the ball.
The quarterback's lateral pass was intercepted by the defense.
The team practiced lateral passes during their training session.

sense.definition: to initiate play by quickly moving the ball into play from a stationary position

examples.x: In American football, the quarterback snaps the ball to start each play.
The soccer player snapped the ball into play with a quick pass to her teammate.
The rugby player snapped the ball out of the scrum to the waiting hands of the fly-half.
The goalie snapped the ball into play with a powerful kick downfield.

sense.definition: (primarily in ball games) to kick or hit the ball with such force that sends it flying forward

examples.x: With a strong kick, he drove the ball past the goalkeeper and into the net.
His baseball swing allowed him to drive the ball into the outfield.
The soccer player managed to drive the ball into the top corner of the goal.
She used her powerful swing to drive the ball over the net in tennis.
The golfer drove the ball straight down the fairway with precision.

sense.definition: the act of capturing something that has been thrown through the air

examples.x: The baseball player made an impressive catch in the outfield.
She made a swift catch, saving her phone from falling off the table.
With lightning-fast reflexes, he made a spectacular catch during the game.
The goalkeeper's incredible catch prevented the opposing team from scoring a goal.
I managed to make a last-minute catch, grabbing the falling book before it hit the ground.
